title: Wetland Functions and E-Waste Filtration
description: A diagram illustrating the natural filtration process of a wetland ecosystem, showing how it can trap and break down contaminants from electronic waste leaching into the water system.
elements: Surface Water Input (Contaminated with heavy metals from e-waste)
relationships: Contaminated water flows into the wetland
Vegetation slows water flow and absorbs some contaminants
Soil particles and organic matter trap and bind heavy metals (e.g., Pb, Hg, Cd)
Microbes break down certain organic pollutants from e-waste
Filtered, cleaner water is released from the wetland
